properties.areaDesc: Delaware, IN; Hamilton, IN; Hancock, IN; Henry, IN; Madison, IN; Marion, IN; Randolph, IN; Rush, IN
properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued June 7 at 9:22PM EDT until June 8 at 12:00AM EDT by NWS Indianapolis IN
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...Southeastern Hamilton County in central Indiana...
Hancock County in central Indiana...
Southern Madison County in central Indiana...
Northeastern Marion County in central Indiana...
Northern Rush County in central Indiana...
Southwestern Delaware County in east central Indiana...
Henry County in east central Indiana...
Southwestern Randolph County in east central Indiana...

* WHEN...Until midnight EDT tonight.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 922 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing in the advisory
area. Between 2.5 and 4 inches of rain have fallen.
- Flooding impacts will continue, but no additional rainfall is
expected.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Indianapolis, Anderson, Fishers, Lawrence, Greenfield, New
Castle, Cumberland, McCordsville, Pendleton, Fortville,
Chesterfield, Ingalls, Knightstown, Edgewood, Daleville,
Meridian Hills, Carthage, Spiceland, Shirley and Markleville.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
properties.instruction:
Be aware of your surroundings and do not drive on flooded roads.

Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law
enforcement and request they pass this information to the National
Weather Service when you can do so safely.

NWSheadline: FLOOD ADVISORY IN EFFECT UNTIL MIDNIGHT EDT TONIGHT

title: Current watches, warnings, and advisories for Indiana
